decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Previous iterations likely exhausted simple conductivity gains or hit diminishing returns. Following the strategy note to use coordinated multi-variable changes, I will simultaneously increase the heat spreader's width and height. This expands the thermal cross-section and lateral spreading area more aggressively than material tuning alone, targeting the high-severity violation.

changes.reason: Maximize spreader width to the domain limit (1.0m) to enhance lateral heat distribution away from the chip center.
Increase spreader height by 50% (within 1.5x limit) to provide a larger vertical conduction path and thermal mass.

expected_effects: Reduction of chip peak temperature below 85.0°C
Lower thermal resistance between chip and boundary conditions
risk_notes: Geometric changes may approach design domain boundaries; ensure no overlap with other components occurs (y0=0.32 + h=0.15 = 0.47 < 0.5 limit).
Aggressive geometry changes might alter flow paths if this were a conjugate problem, though less critical in pure conduction.
If this step fails to satisfy constraints, the next logical step would be increasing base_material conductivity.
